message.content:
@startuml
skinparam sequenceMessageAlign center

box "host" #LightBlue
    participant MultiDisplayService as host_MultiDisplayService
end box

box "" #White
    participant IMultipleDisplaysUiController as rcv_IMultipleDisplaysUiController
    participant IMultipleDisplaysDelegate as rcv_IMultipleDisplaysDelegate
end box

box "controller" #LightGreen
    participant DisplaysSwapViewController as controller_DisplaysSwapViewController
    participant DisplaysSwapPresenter as controller_DisplaysSwapPresenter
end box


' Display Added Flow
rcv_IMultipleDisplaysUiController ->> host_MultiDisplayService : onDisplayAdded()
activate host_MultiDisplayService
note right of host_MultiDisplayService: Update display list\nand notify delegates
deactivate host_MultiDisplayService

host_MultiDisplayService --> rcv_IMultipleDisplaysUiController : updateDisplayInfos(displayList)
activate rcv_IMultipleDisplaysUiController
note left of rcv_IMultipleDisplaysUiController: Refresh UI with new\ndisplay configuration
deactivate rcv_IMultipleDisplaysUiController



' Swap Action Initiated by User in Controller Layer
participant user <<actor>> 
user -[hidden]> controller_DisplaysSwapViewController 

group swap displays action initiated from view layer [swap] {
  note over controller_DisplaysSwapViewController,user  
      The View Controller receives an event to initiate swapping two monitors.\nIt calls into Presenter which handles business logic for monitor reordering/swapping.
   end note
   
   
     ' Step A – Call presenter method responsible for handling this specific case/action type related directly towards changing order/positioning among multiple active screens/displays connected system-wide across all users logged-in currently using same machine instance at once time period during their session duration until logout happens automatically after certain timeout limit reached due idle state detected via background service running continuously monitoring activity levels reported back periodically through network connections established between client devices accessing shared resources hosted remotely elsewhere outside local area networks where most personal computers reside today mostly anyway unless you're working inside corporate environment having strict security policies enforced strictly everywhere including home offices too sometimes even schools universities colleges etcetera ad infinitum... 
    
        rect rgb(240, 255, 240)    
            alt if there exists more than one physical screen attached already then proceed further else abort operation immediately without doing anything whatsoever because nothing needs changed here obviously since single-monitor setups don't require any kind special treatment beyond what default behavior provides out-of-the-box basically speaking generally universally applicable regardless
